title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends on fuel transition, shipyard actions, government, ports, financing and industry players. There are growing calls for a carbon tax on shipping emissions. Read More

Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
Weaker sentiment in freight rates continued in the last week of June, however, iron ore price hit a one-week high on China's demand hopes amid easing Covid-19 curbs and President Xi Jinping's pledge to take more effective measures to achieve the country's economic and social development goals.

children.title: China's coal production has remained quite strong
section_path: China's coal production has remained quite strong
children.section_path_text: China's coal production has remained quite strong
children.summary: As we have discussed in Commodore's recent Weekly Dry Bulk Reports and Weekly China Reports, China's coal production in May totaled 367.8 million tons.

children.title: Looking forward to Q3
children.summary: Having reached the halfway point of the year, we look at what has been driving the recent drawdown in Capesize rates and what may be in store for the rest of the year.

children.title: Equities rebounded somewhat at the back end of last week in what was mostly a relief rally
section_path: Equities rebounded somewhat at the back end of last week in what was mostly a relief rally
children.section_path_text: Equities rebounded somewhat at the back end of last week in what was mostly a relief rally
children.summary: Far from being considered a full recovery, last week illustrates how high market volatility is and how fragile sentiment remains.

children.title: Global crude steel production has now contracted on a year-on-year basis for nine straight months

children.section_path_text: Global crude steel production has now contracted on a year-on-year basis for nine straight months
children.summary: Global crude steel production totaled 169.5 million tons in May.

children.title: Russian oil in-transit is hitting record highs, driven by the need to send barrels to the East, and is inspiring new shipping practices

children.section_path_text: Russian oil in-transit is hitting record highs, driven by the need to send barrels to the East, and is inspiring new shipping practices
children.summary: Russia's oil exports over Q2 have been pretty stable, a contrast to Russian natural gas flows into Europe.
